gap_analysis: The single biggest blind spot across all 5 verdicts is the interaction between the DOJ criminal probe and the MA billing model that underlies the entire bull thesis. The Narrative seat flags the probe as 'OPEN, no timeline' but treats it as a background noise item. The Fundamental seat focuses on MCR normalization without modeling what happens to revenue if a DOJ settlement mandates structural changes to MA diagnostic-coding practices — which are the mechanism that inflated revenue in the first place. The Technical seat ignores it entirely. Critically: if the DOJ requires UNH to unwind risk-adjustment coding practices that were labeled fraudulent, MCR normalization becomes structurally impossible because the prior MCR baseline was itself built on inflated revenue numerators. None of the 5 seats prices this feedback loop — a settlement could simultaneously force lower revenue recognition AND higher reported medical costs, hitting the P&L from both sides.
tail_risk: DOJ escalates to formal criminal indictment of UnitedHealth Group or its MA subsidiary for systematic risk-adjustment fraud (pattern matching Columbia/HCA-style charges). Probability: 6%. Trigger path: DOJ has had the probe running for multiple quarters with civil AND criminal tracks open simultaneously — dual-track probes historically resolve as either a large NPA/DPA or escalate to charges when cooperation stalls. A company-level indictment would cause CMS to threaten or suspend UNH's MA contract eligibility (analogous to federal contractor debarment risk), immediately impairing $200B+ in MA revenue. Expected loss magnitude: -45 to -60% from current price ($426), implying loss of $192–$256 per share. At current position that is a -$1,959 to -$2,613 realized loss on the Roth holding. [LIVE — DOJ dual-track probe confirmed by Narrative seat citing fxleaders 2026-04-08]
historical_analog: Humana (HUM) 2023-2024 [MEM]. Structural match: (1) V-shaped price recovery to near 52w high after a COVID-era MLR disruption; (2) analyst consensus crowded bullish with multiple upgrades citing 'normalization'; (3) insiders and large funds quietly reducing — Berkshire had also reduced HUM exposure in the same period; (4) MA rate environment gave false confidence (CMS 2024 rate update initially read as supportive). Outcome: when Q1 2024 earnings revealed MLR deterioration rather than normalization, HUM collapsed from ~$550 in Feb 2024 to below $200 by late 2024, a drawdown exceeding 60%. The parallel to UNH is direct: the market is again betting on MCR normalization that has not yet been confirmed in reported results, at a valuation that prices in a recovery that could reverse on a single bad print. ⚠️[MEM-only]
invalidation_a: (a) PRICE LEVEL: Three consecutive daily closes below $395 (the technical stop cited by the Technical seat) — this invalidates the '2-week base continuation' setup and signals the breakout above the 52w-high is failing, increasing probability of a reversal to the prior consolidation range near $340-$360. [LIVE — Technical seat stop level]
invalidation_b: (b) EARNINGS/RATIO THRESHOLD: Q2 2026 reported MCR (Medical Cost Ratio) at or above 85.5% on July 16 earnings — this invalidates the MCR normalization thesis that is the core fundamental catalyst. Current reported 83.9% needs to hold or improve; a deterioration above 85.5% signals the Q1 'improvement' was noise rather than trend, and consensus EPS estimates of $4.84 (+18.6%) would require a severe downward revision. [LIVE — MCR 83.9% cited by Narrative seat, barchart 2026-07-01]
invalidation_c: (c) FILING/LEGAL EVENT: Any SEC Form 8-K or DOJ press release announcing formal criminal charges, a superseding indictment, or a deferred prosecution agreement with admission of wrongdoing against UnitedHealth Group Inc. or OptumInsight — this invalidates the assumption that the DOJ probe is a background regulatory risk rather than an existential MA-franchise threat. Also invalidating: any CMS communication placing UNH's MA contracts under enhanced oversight or probationary status as a result of the probe. [LIVE — DOJ probe cited by Narrative seat, fxleaders 2026-04-08]
portfolio_tail_stress: At 1.0% book weight ($4,354 current value at $426.54): a -30% move to ~$299 = -$1,306 realized loss; a -50% move to ~$213 = -$2,177 realized loss. In absolute terms these losses are immaterial to the $444,748 Fidelity book — they represent 0.3% and 0.5% of total book respectively. However, at 5.1% of the invested sleeve these moves are felt at the sleeve level. More important: the Roth IRA wrapper means losses cannot be harvested for tax benefit and gains cannot be distributed tax-efficiently until retirement — the asymmetry of a permanent Roth loss (no tax offset) vs a permanent Roth gain (tax-free) argues for tighter risk management on position sizing than the 1.0% book weight alone implies.

verdict_new_money: WATCH — the average analyst price target of $413 is already 3.2% BELOW the current price of $426.54, meaning the consensus sell-side has no room for new buyers; entering here means paying above what 26 analysts collectively think it is worth, with the Q2 MCR catalyst on July 16 as the only near-term binary that could justify the premium. [LIVE — Sentiment seat]
verdict_holding: HOLD — at 1.0% book weight the position is immaterial, the Roth wrapper eliminates tax-loss harvesting value of trimming, and the cost basis of $342.84 still offers a 24.4% cushion before returning to breakeven; the rational move is to hold through the July 16 Q2 earnings print and reassess against invalidation conditions (b) and (c) before adding or reducing.
controlling_factor: The controlling factor for both verdicts is the July 16 Q2 earnings print: a confirmed MCR at or below 83.9% with no DOJ escalation validates the recovery thesis and opens the technical breakout; a MCR miss above 85% or any DOJ development converts the 'normalization hope' valuation into a value trap at P/E 32.
